title: Entryway Console
summary: Need a fun stylish storage console for the entryway?
description: Need a fun stylish storage console for the entryway? Check out this fun build.

steps.title: Start Building Console Legs
steps.body:
Attach the 2x2 support (12 1/2") to the 2x2s (33 3/4") using a Kreg Jig K4 Pocket Hole System on a 1 1/2" setting, with 2 1/2" Pocket Hole Screws to attach along with Wood Glue.
Note: The pocket holes on the bottom support should face up on the top support.

steps.title: Build Console Leg
steps.body:
Attach the bottom 2x2 support (12 1/2") to the 2x2s (33 3/4") using a Kreg Jig K4 Pocket Hole System on a 1 1/2" setting, with 2 1/2" Pocket Hole Screws to attach along with Wood Glue.
Note: The pocket holes on the supports should face down on the bottom support.

steps.title: Attach Top Console Legs
steps.body:
Attach the 2x2 (44 1/2") supports to the console legs using a Kreg Jig K4 Pocket Hole System on a 1 1/2" setting, with 2 1/2" Pocket Hole Screws to attach along with Wood Glue.
Note: The pocket holes on the supports should face up on the support.

steps.title: Attach Bottom Console Legs
steps.body:
Attach the bottom 2x2 (44 1/2") supports to the console legs using a Kreg Jig K4 Pocket Hole System on a 1 1/2" setting, with 2 1/2" Pocket Hole Screws to attach along with Wood Glue.
Note: The pocket holes on the supports should face down on the support.

steps.title: Add Side Supports
steps.body:
Attach the four 2x2 (28") supports to the console legs using a brad nailer and 2" brad nails.
Two 2x2s should be placed on both sides.

steps.title: Attach Back Supports
steps.body: Attach the two 2x2 (32") with a brad nailer and 2" brad nails. The top 2x2 should be cut at 30 degrees and bottom angle should be cut at 60 degrees.

steps.title: Attach Console Top
steps.body: Attach the project panel top to the completed console bottom using 2" brad nails.
